title: leptolobium clandestinum
intro: Leptolobium clandestinum, also known as the secret tree, is a plant species that belongs to the Fabaceae family and is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Leptolobium clandestinum include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic. Leptolobium clandestinum is traditionally prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves, bark, and roots are used to make herbal teas, infusions, and poultices.
